title: mimo2codex Desktop
tagline: Run mimo2codex in the background. Tray icon. One-click admin UI. No terminal needed.

notReleasedYet.title: Desktop builds not yet released
notReleasedYet.detail: We haven't shipped a desktop release yet. For now, install the CLI:

noMatchPlatform.title: Your platform isn't auto-detected
noMatchPlatform.detail: Pick a build from "Other platforms" below.

bg.title: Runs in the background
bg.detail: No terminal window to keep open. Closes to tray, not the dock.

tray.title: One-click admin
tray.detail: Open settings, view logs, restart sidecar — all from the tray menu.

autostart.title: Launch at boot (optional)
autostart.detail: Toggle "Start on system boot" from the tray; ready before you open Codex.

macSteps.title: macOS install & first launch (4 steps)
macSteps.intro: Desktop builds aren't code-signed yet, so macOS Gatekeeper blocks the .app extracted from a downloaded .zip with "App is damaged, can't be opened". Follow these 4 steps:
macSteps.s1: Double-click the downloaded .zip in your Downloads folder — Finder unzips it to mimo2codex.app.
macSteps.s2: Drag mimo2codex.app into the Applications folder (/Applications).
macSteps.s3: Open Terminal, paste the line below and press Enter:
macSteps.s3Hint: (This clears the browser-download quarantine flag. Without it, the next step would fail with "is damaged". The command produces no output — that's normal.)
macSteps.s4: Double-click mimo2codex.app in Applications. Look for the m2 icon in your menu bar to open the settings panel.

security.title: First launch will show a security warning
security.mac: macOS fallback: if you skipped the 4 steps above and double-clicking the app shows "is damaged, can't be opened", run xattr -cr /Applications/mimo2codex.app  once. (Right-click → Open works on some older macOS versions but not all; the xattr command always works.)
security.sha: These warnings are expected — desktop builds aren't code-signed yet. Verify the SHA256 above matches the file you downloaded.
security.uninstall: Uninstall: macOS — drag mimo2codex.app from /Applications to Trash; optionally wipe configs with `rm -rf ~/Library/Application\ Support/mimo2codex-desktop`. Windows — Settings → Apps → mimo2codex → Uninstall.

cliHint: Prefer the command line? npm install -g mimo2codex
